commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the model-level `api` and `baseUrl` overrides from the `gpt-5.3-codex` OpenAI catalog entry and updates provider tests for that catalog behavior.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +8. Total +6 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a concrete v2026.6.1 Docker/API-key/stale-plugin path, and current source plus the release tag show the model is forced to `openai-chatgpt-responses`; I did not rerun the live gateway repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review should focus on accepting the auth-routing default change and requiring patched OpenClaw real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: :bug: fix(openai): remove chatgpt-responses transport override from gpt-5.3-codex catalog entry
target.body:
## Summary

The static catalog entry for `gpt-5.3-codex` in `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json` hardcoded `api: "openai-chatgpt-responses"` and `baseUrl: "https://chatgpt.com/backend-api"`, forcing all users through the ChatGPT backend transport. This requires OAuth authentication -- users with standard OpenAI API keys cannot use it.

Removed the transport overrides so the model inherits the provider defaults (`openai-responses` + `api.openai.com/v1`), restoring the v2026.5.18 behavior where `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` worked with standard API keys.

OAuth/Codex users are unaffected -- dynamic model resolution via `shouldResolveDynamicModelThroughCodex` handles the ChatGPT routing based on provider config, not the static catalog entry.

Reviewers should focus on whether removing the static transport overrides affects any other code path that reads `api`/`baseUrl` from the `gpt-5.3-codex` catalog template (verified safe -- `augmentModelCatalog` spreads the template but `normalizeCodexTransport` rewrites transport fields independently).

## Linked context

Closes #91710

Related #91711 (companion PR: classifies harness provider mismatch as `format` error instead of `unknown`)

Related #61982 (prior codex reasoning_effort regression fix)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` was completely unusable with standard OpenAI API keys since v2026.6.1 due to the static catalog entry forcing ChatGPT backend transport.
- Real environment tested: macOS host with OpenAI API key, calling `api.openai.com/v1/responses` directly with model `gpt-5.3-codex`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
curl -s https://api.openai.com/v1/responses \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "gpt-5.3-codex", "input": "Say hello in exactly 3 words.", "max_output_tokens": 50}'
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture of live API response confirming gpt-5.3-codex works on standard OpenAI API):

```
$ curl -s https://api.openai.com/v1/responses \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model": "gpt-5.3-codex", "input": "Say hello in exactly 3 words.", "max_output_tokens": 50}' \
    | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('model:', d.get('model')); print('status:', d.get('status')); print('id:', d.get('id')); [print('text:', c.get('text','')[:200]) for item in d.get('output',[]) for c in item.get('content',[])]"

model: gpt-5.3-codex
status: completed
id: resp_0efaee5cacffa630006a2825e39b9481919f0d2ae9a26a19c6
text: Hello there, friend.
```

This confirms `gpt-5.3-codex` exists on the standard OpenAI Responses API and responds successfully with a standard API key -- no OAuth/ChatGPT backend needed.

Before the fix, the static catalog entry would route this model to `chatgpt.com/backend-api` which rejects standard API keys, causing every request to fail with a harness provider mismatch (see #91710 for detailed gateway logs).

- Observed result after fix: `gpt-5.3-codex` responds successfully via `api.openai.com/v1/responses` with a standard API key. The model returns `status: completed` and valid output text.
- What was not tested: End-to-end OpenClaw gateway flow with the patched catalog (would require rebuilding the Docker image). OAuth/Codex users were not tested (they use a dynamic resolution path unaffected by this change).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live API call was made directly to OpenAI, not through the OpenClaw gateway. The gateway routing logic was verified via unit tests that assert the catalog entry no longer has `openai-chatgpt-responses` transport.

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts
# 37 passed (including new regression test)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-provider.test.ts
# 6 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/provider-policy-api.test.ts
# 1 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/index.test.ts
# 13 passed
```

Added 1 new regression test asserting `gpt-5.3-codex` catalog entry has no `api` or `baseUrl` overrides.

Updated 1 existing test that asserted `codexModel?.baseUrl` was `chatgpt.com` -- this model no longer has ChatGPT transport in the static catalog.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes -- `openai/gpt-5.3-codex` now routes through the standard OpenAI API (`api.openai.com/v1`) instead of the ChatGPT backend (`chatgpt.com/backend-api`). This restores the v2026.5.18 behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No -- OAuth/Codex users are unaffected (dynamic resolution handles their routing). API key users get the correct endpoint.

What is the highest-risk area? The `gpt-5.3-codex` entry is used as a template by `augmentModelCatalog` for synthesizing Codex OAuth model entries.

How is that risk mitigated? Verified that `buildOpenAISyntheticCatalogEntry` only uses metadata fields (`cost`, `contextWindow`, `reasoning`, etc.) from the template, and `normalizeCodexTransport` independently rewrites transport fields for Codex models.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? N/A -- first submission.
target.author: a-tokyo
